findings.title: Upgradeable deployment has no initialization path, leaving owner and core state permanently unset

best_agent_match.title: Upgradeable token has no initializer, leaving ownership and the rebase factor permanently unset

findings.title: Unassigned `_mainPool` makes ordinary transfers revert via zero-address pool sync

best_agent_match.title: Uninitialized `_mainPool` makes ordinary transfers revert on address(0) ERC20 calls

findings.title: Spot-balance rebase logic is flash-loan manipulable and applies an uncapped quadratic mint on buys

best_agent_match.title: Flash-loan manipulable spot balances drive an uncapped quadratic rebase mint

findings.title: Liquidity reserve migration burns and skims treasury funds because it is not performed taxlessly

best_agent_match.title: Liquidity reserve migration burns and skims the reserve because it is not executed taxlessly

findings.title: No code path can ever mark the presale as finished, so all transfers remain permanently disabled

best_agent_match.title: No function to mark presale as done

rejected_candidates.title: Uninitialized presale address allows anyone to mint tokens
rejected_candidates.reason: False positive: no transaction can originate from `address(0)`. A zero `_presaleCon` makes `mint()` unreachable, not publicly callable; that bricking is covered by F-001.

rejected_candidates.title: getFactor() calculation broken - token balances become incorrect after transactions
rejected_candidates.reason: The claim misunderstands the rebase accounting model. Large-balance conversions are intentionally handled via the factor and `_largeTotal`; the concrete bug here is missing initialization, already captured in F-001.

rejected_candidates.title: setTaxless() lacks access control - anyone can set taxless mode
rejected_candidates.reason: Incorrect: `setTaxless(bool)` is explicitly protected by the `onlyTaxless` modifier.

rejected_candidates.title: Division by zero in getFactor() if totalSupply becomes zero
rejected_candidates.reason: Too speculative as a standalone issue and not needed to explain realistic harm; the concrete deploy-time bricking from missing initialization is already captured in F-001.

rejected_candidates.title: createTokenPool uses block.timestamp as deadline - can fail immediately
rejected_candidates.reason: Using `block.timestamp` as a same-transaction router deadline is standard and does not by itself create a realistic exploit or loss scenario.

rejected_candidates.title: reassignTranche allows assigning to zero address
rejected_candidates.reason: Privileged misconfiguration/footgun only; no permissionless exploit or protocol-level failure is introduced.

rejected_candidates.title: silentSyncPair can be called on arbitrary addresses
rejected_candidates.reason: Public calls mainly expose the caller to a revert; the meaningful protocol issue is the internal use of zero `_mainPool`, captured in F-002.

rejected_candidates.title: addSupportedPool does not validate pairToken address
rejected_candidates.reason: Owner-only misconfiguration risk without a permissionless exploit path; not a reportable protocol bug on its own.

rejected_candidates.title: getUpdatedPoolCounters uses address(this) instead of pool address for token balance
rejected_candidates.reason: `IERC20(address(this)).balanceOf(pool)` is the correct way to read this token's pool balance. Problems from unsupported pools are already covered by F-002.

rejected_candidates.title: Using outdated Solidity version ^0.6.12
rejected_candidates.reason: Generic informational note, not a concrete vulnerability in this codebase.

rejected_candidates.title: No zero address validation in setLiquidityReserve and setStabilizer
rejected_candidates.reason: `AddressUpgradeable.isContract(reserve)` already rejects `address(0)`, so the specific zero-address claim is unsupported.
